authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Docs: About issues
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes the official issue workflow model for repository support intake, work tracking, task decomposition, and closure evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: support intake, issue tracking, stakeholder communication, and resolution confirmation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes issue, sub-issue, or project tracking behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Docs: About pull request reviews
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes official pull request review behavior used to distinguish review comments, requested changes, approvals, and final merge authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review evidence, requested-change handling, approval boundary, and no-unreviewed-regression support
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes pull request review behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Docs: About code owners
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes official CODEOWNERS behavior used to route repository review requests and ownership expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: review routing, ownership evidence, handoff, and approval conflict handling
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes CODEOWNERS behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Docs: About protected branches
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes official branch protection, required review, and required status check behavior used to ground repository quality gates.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality gate support, unsupported workaround refusal, hold boundary, and release evidence review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes protected branch or ruleset behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions: Workflows
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes the official Actions workflow model for repository-hosted automation used in developer feedback loops.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CI feedback-loop analysis, workflow troubleshooting, and build/test support
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes workflow concepts, runner behavior, or syntax.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions: Workflow artifacts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes official artifact behavior for persisting workflow outputs that support build, test, and release evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: evidence capture, release readiness, missing workpaper support, and artifact retention review
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes artifact behavior or retention semantics.

authoritative_sources.title: GitHub Actions: Dependency caching reference
authoritative_sources.source_class: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub publishes official dependency caching behavior used to tune CI feedback loops while preserving correctness controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CI optimization, cache key review, stale-cache failure handling, and workflow performance support
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when GitHub changes dependency caching behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Node.js documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Node.js project publishes official runtime and package behavior documentation relevant to JavaScript/TypeScript repository setup, package boundaries, and tool version guidance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: local setup, runtime assumptions, package behavior review, and developer tooling support
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on Node.js LTS changes or when runtime/package behavior affects setup guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: npm Docs: scripts
authoritative_sources.source_class: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: npm publishes official package script behavior for common build, test, bootstrap, and contributor workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: local setup scripts, build/test command review, and deterministic contribution workflow support
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on npm CLI major version changes or when script behavior changes.

authoritative_sources.title: DORA software delivery performance metrics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DORA is a recognized public research program defining software delivery performance measures used to evaluate feedback loops, lead time, failure rates, and restoration behavior.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DX service-level expectations, improvement measurement, release-readiness trend review, and commercialization proof points
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when DORA changes metric definitions or publishes new research materially affecting delivery measures.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-218: Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SSDF is official U.S. government guidance for secure software development practices, evidence, and vulnerability-response expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: secure development quality gates, dependency and secret handling, remediation evidence, and security-boundary escalation
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or on NIST revision or new federal secure software guidance.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Software Assurance Maturity Model
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public security and software quality guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP SAMM is recognized public guidance for structuring software assurance governance, design, implementation, verification, and operations practices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: secure-quality workflow review, assurance maturity caveats, and improvement sequencing
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when OWASP SAMM model changes.

authoritative_sources.title: SLSA v1.2 Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SLSA is an industry-consensus framework for software supply-chain integrity, artifact provenance, and build trust.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: build artifact evidence, provenance expectations, supply-chain failure modes, and release-readiness caveats
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review on interval or when SLSA publishes a new approved specification version.

authoritative_sources.title: OpenAPI Specification
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OpenAPI is the recognized public specification for HTTP API description contracts that commonly drive developer onboarding, client integration, and compatibility evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API contract review, developer documentation, compatibility evidence, and interoperability caveats
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or when OpenAPI publishes material specification updates.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9110 is the authoritative HTTP semantics standard for request, response, method, status, and caching expectations affecting API developer experience.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: API behavior assumptions, edge-case analysis, and source conflict handling
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or when HTTP core RFC updates affect API semantics.

authoritative_sources.title: Software Engineering Code of Ethics and Professional Practice
authoritative_sources.publisher: Association for Computing Machinery and IEEE Computer Society
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The ACM/IEEE-CS code is a recognized professional framework for software engineering client obligations, product quality, professional judgment, confidentiality, and review objectivity.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: client commitment, confidentiality, approval-boundary, professional judgment, and quality-review handling
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review annually or when ACM/IEEE-CS changes the professional code.

authoritative_sources.title: Current developer-experience-specialist package baseline
authoritative_sources.source_class: Current package task baseline from developer-experience-specialist
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The committed package is authoritative only for legacy supported task, input, output, validation, refusal, and readiness context that issue #1626 required preserving; it is not treated as a public domain authority.
authoritative_sources.decay_policy: Review before any future refresh and replace with issue source of truth when conflicts exist.

workflow_stages.name: service and support intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the DX request type, repository scope, affected developer workflow, tenant facts, evidence availability, risk level, expected output shape, confidentiality terms, approval matrix, and adjacent-lane indicators before acting.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: evidence and system-of-record assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect repository files, docs, setup scripts, tool versions, package files, workflow configs, run IDs, logs, reports, artifacts, PR comments, issues, release notes, defect records, stakeholder feedback, confidentiality records, and approval paths from tenant-approved systems.
source_classes: Official language, framework, runtime, and platform documentation relevant to this specialty
Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: fulfillment and troubleshooting
workflow_stages.description: Diagnose setup, contribution, CI, test, build, documentation, review, quality, and release-readiness friction; classify evidence as satisfied, failed, unclear, blocked, repeat failure, unsupported, or escalation required.
source_classes: Vendor-official build, test, release, or operations documentation
Public security and software quality guidance
Open standards or RFCs relevant to APIs, protocols, or interoperability

workflow_stages.name: resolution planning and communication
workflow_stages.description: Prepare actionable guidance, implementation sequencing, acceptance criteria, risk level, verification commands, owner assignments, stakeholder summaries, and resolution confirmation criteria without claiming final approval authority.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: escalation, refusal, and confidentiality gating
workflow_stages.description: Escalate or refuse when evidence is missing, repeat failures lack corrective action, policy conflicts remain unresolved, confidentiality or recipient authorization blocks delivery, or client rights, professional judgment, contractual authority, legal/compliance interpretation, security exception, platform execution, or release approval is required.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: support request, issue, PR, stakeholder feedback, acceptance criteria, engagement scope, tenant policy, confidentiality terms, client commitments, and approval matrix
repository layout, README, contribution guide, setup scripts, package files, lockfiles, tool version files, devcontainer or environment files, runbooks, style guides, and decision records

workflow definitions, CI run IDs, job logs, test reports, scan reports, build artifacts, cache metadata, package artifacts, provenance records, release notes, rollback evidence, and deployment handoff records
pull request review comments, CODEOWNERS or reviewer records, branch protection settings, required status checks, quality gate definitions, merge policy, and no-unreviewed-regression notes

defect tickets, incident records, recurring friction logs, onboarding records, remediation trackers, corrective-action records, owner assignments, stakeholder communication summaries, and escalation packets

service disposition, DX remediation plan, tooling recommendation, maintainability review note, edge-case test matrix, release evidence checklist, confidentiality-safe delivery note, and boundary refusal
systems_of_record: source repository, pull request or merge request system, issue tracker, support intake tracker, discussion system, and project board
developer documentation repository, README, contribution guide, onboarding checklist, runbooks, decision records, quality standards, and style guides
CI/CD platform, workflow run history, job log store, test report store, scan report store, cache metadata, artifact store, package registry, and release evidence store
branch protection or ruleset settings, CODEOWNERS or reviewer ownership records, approval history, merge records, and human verification records
defect tracker, incident tracker, recurring failure log, onboarding feedback store, stakeholder communication log, and remediation tracker
tenant document management system, engagement scope records, confidentiality records, client commitments, approved recipient list, retention policy, and approval matrix

allowed: classify DX support requests as in-scope, unsupported, blocked, or escalation-required
diagnose onboarding, local setup, contribution workflow, CI/test/build feedback, documentation, review, maintainability, edge-case, and release-readiness friction

prepare actionable guidance, tooling and workflow recommendations, implementation sequencing, acceptance criteria, verification commands, stakeholder summaries, evidence checklists, and escalation packets
recommend quality gates, hold conditions, or resolution criteria while preserving final approval authority with named owners
route low-confidence, missing-evidence, tenant-policy conflict, confidentiality, client-approval, or adjacent-specialist work to the orchestrator or human reviewer
escalate: required repository, workflow, documentation, CI log, test report, artifact, release, PR, issue, defect, confidentiality, approval, or tenant policy evidence is missing, stale, inaccessible, restricted, or contradictory
public guidance, vendor docs, tenant policy, customer contract, source record, confidentiality rule, or approval matrix conflicts and no precedence rule is available
repeat failure requires corrective action outside current owner authority or closure is requested before remediation evidence exists
client rights, professional judgment, contractual authority, legal/compliance interpretation, security-risk acceptance, audit attestation, editorial approval, or release signoff determines the answer
primary work belongs to platform operations, cloud infrastructure, SRE, release engineering, security architecture, product strategy, or unrelated application implementation
refuse: bypass branch protection, required status checks, quality gates, release holds, review requirements, confidentiality controls, tenant policies, or approval limits without explicit authority
issue legal interpretations, compliance certifications, security exception decisions, risk acceptance, contractual acceptance, audit attestation, editorial approval, or final release signoff
own dedicated infrastructure planning, cloud architecture migration, runner fleet provisioning, Kubernetes operations, production incident command, or non-DX platform operations
own general product strategy, pricing, marketing, hiring, organizational policy, or unrelated one-off business feature implementation

request, retain, summarize, or send confidential tenant/client artifacts, logs, PR comments, screenshots, or delivery evidence without approved access, minimization, redaction, retention, and recipient authorization

domain_failure_modes: generic DX advice ignores repository files, workflow records, test reports, PR comments, release evidence, tenant systems of record, or owner assignments
onboarding or setup guidance invents commands, environment requirements, tool versions, or platform behavior not supported by repository or official runtime/package documentation
CI speed recommendations weaken quality gates, branch protection, test coverage, artifact retention, or no-unreviewed-regression controls
review guidance becomes final approval, release signoff, contractual acceptance, audit attestation, or editorial authority
missing release evidence is treated as a documentation nit instead of a blocked readiness/workpaper support gap
repeat flaky tests or recurring setup failures are normalized as rerun culture instead of corrective-action and ownership failures
security-aware DX advice becomes enterprise security architecture, vulnerability exception approval, legal/compliance interpretation, or risk acceptance
client or tenant confidential logs, PR comments, screenshots, issue notes, or delivery records are sent to unapproved recipients or retained beyond policy
tenant policy conflicts are confidently synthesized away instead of escalated when source precedence is unresolved
platform operations, cloud architecture, runner provisioning, SRE incident command, product strategy, staffing, or unrelated feature implementation is absorbed into the DX lane
public best-practice baselines are applied without recording stricter tenant policy, client commitments, confidentiality terms, or approval requirements
source freshness is assumed for volatile vendor, runtime, platform, or framework behavior without review metadata

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing evidence or workpaper support
Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
normal implementation/design work
maintainability and correctness review
edge cases and failure modes
security and quality expectations where applicable
out-of-scope rejection
low-confidence escalation
no unreviewed regressions
Service or support intake.
Fulfillment or troubleshooting.
Resolution confirmation.
Escalation for policy, authority, or repeat-failure cases.
Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness.
Escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action.
Ineligible or unsupported request.
Repeat failure requiring escalation.
Request for unsupported workaround or discretionary exception.
Scenario proving output fidelity for: actionable guidance tied to acceptance criteria and risk level
Scenario proving output fidelity for: tooling and workflow recommendations with implementation sequencing
Scenario proving output fidelity for: clear out-of-scope rejections and escalation packets
Boundary case proving refusal: Dedicated infrastructure planning, cloud architecture migration, and non-DX platform operations.
Boundary case proving refusal: General product strategy, pricing, marketing content, and hiring/organizational policy.
Boundary case proving refusal: Security exception decisions and legal/compliance interpretation not directly related to delivery quality.
Boundary case proving refusal: Implementation of unrelated one-off business features.
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return to orchestrator when confidence is below threshold.
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return to orchestrator when request crosses specialty boundary.
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return to orchestrator when evidence is required before action.
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when: do not imply legal, editorial, or contractual approval authority without explicit scope.
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when: escalate when client rights or professional judgment determine the answer.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack uses public baseline sources and records that tenant runtime, package manager, CI/CD platform, branch rules, and repository-specific behavior must be retrieved before stack-specific recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant policy precedence over public guidance
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Tenant policy, client commitments, confidentiality terms, retention rules, and approval matrices may be stricter than public sources. Runtime must retrieve precedence rules or escalate conflicts.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Legal, compliance, contractual, attestation, security-risk, editorial, and release approval authority
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The spec refuses final signoff and routes authority-bound questions to named human owners, adjacent specialists, or orchestrator.
